summary: P1-B's copied-command execution boundary is present in both plan workflows, but the command-shaped detector is not adversarially normalized for case, whitespace, wrappers, shell metacharacters, unicode/escape tricks, or control characters.

findingDetails.title: Command-shaped detector is not adversarially normalized
findingDetails.evidence: Both plan workflows forbid executing or rerunning copied activeFindings[].scopeProof commands and only allow actions from verified repo-relative paths, symbols, or independently selected safe commands, but the explicit detector only lists lowercase starts bash|node|npm|git|rm|cat|eval|curl|mv and contains &&, ;, >, or backticks. It does not state trim, case-folding, NUL/control rejection, unicode/escape normalization, bare pipe, <, $(), newlines, or common wrapper binaries such as sh, python, wget, env, sudo, npx, pnpm, and yarn.
findingDetails.recommendation: Keep the blanket no-execute rule and harden all detector wording sites to normalize before detection: trim, reject control/NUL, case-fold ASCII command prefixes, treat escaped/unicode-disguised command text as UNKNOWN, include pipe/substitution/newline metacharacters and wrapper executables, and keep auto/confirm inline scaffold plus writing_rule wording identical.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same-class producer inventory found the P1-B detector at spec_kit_plan_auto.yaml:220 and :576 plus spec_kit_plan_confirm.yaml:226 and :625; adversarial matrix shows lowercase bash/eval/curl starts are caught, while uppercase, leading whitespace, wrappers, bare pipes, $(), unicode/escape, and NUL/control forms are not explicitly covered.

noveltyJustification: The pass validates the prior P1-B no-execute boundary and narrows the remaining issue to detector hardening against adversarial shell-shaped encodings.
traceabilityChecks: P1-B command-shaped/shell-shaped detector adversarial normalization: CONDITIONAL
coverage: Applied R5 checklist by classifying the remaining detector concern as algorithmic/cross-consumer, inventorying both plan workflow producers, checking cross-consumer flow through auto and confirm planning, and enumerating adversarial matrix rows.
ruledOut: P1-B remains open as direct command execution: both plan workflows explicitly forbid executing or rerunning copied scopeProof commands.
